npcs.name: Edda Sorn
npcs.role: Trade Compact agent — local liaison, probable whistleblower
npcs.description: She was sent to Ashford to oversee [[The Trade Compact|Compact]] interests and immediately started noticing things she wasn't supposed to notice. She has been quietly documenting the discrepancy between declared mine output and actual shipments for four months. She approached the party because she doesn't know who else to trust — not [[Governor Harwick Denn]], not the Compact office, and not [[Bram Ketterly]], though she has started to wonder.
npcs.dm_notes: She has documented enough to implicate someone senior in the [[The Trade Compact|Trade Compact]] — she doesn't know the name yet, but the trail is there. If threatened, she has a second copy of her records with a courier in the capital. She will not be silenced easily, and she knows it.

log.note: Edda Sorn contacts the party privately at the inn. She lays out her shipping discrepancy records: declared output versus actual weight shipped, month by month. The gap has been growing for eight months. She needs someone to take this further.

Consequence of Maren Vosk (rival): The Ledger responds to the Keep incident by doubling Ironmask patrols in town and imposing a 'security surcharge' on all merchants. She is escalating, not retreating.
Consequence of Maren Vosk (rival): Ironmask escalation — doubled patrols, new surcharge on merchants.
Consequence of Maren Vosk (rival): The Ledger captured — Ironmask operation collapses.

Edda Sorn presents the shipping discrepancy records to the Trade Compact regional office, backed by Maren Vosk's seized ledgers. The Compact has opened a formal investigation. The Governor has gone quiet. The Factor hasn't been found yet.

npcs.name: Bram Ketterly
npcs.role: Dwarf freight merchant — prisoner at Greystone Keep
npcs.description: He refused to pay the [[The Ironmasks|Ironmask]] toll three months ago on principle. They took his wagon, his cargo, and him. He is currently being held at Greystone Keep as a 'debt collection.' He has information about the [[The Shadow Wing|Shadow Wing]] supply route that he doesn't know he has — and [[Edda Sorn]] has been waiting for someone to go get him.
npcs.dm_notes: The [[The Shadow Wing|Shadow Wing]] couriers he saw wore a specific brooch — a wing etched in silver. He can describe it clearly but doesn't know what it means. He would recognize [[Serev]] on sight if given a physical description to compare against.

log.note: Bram Ketterly is released from the Keep's storage cellar, underfed but uninjured. He remembers faces. He remembers routes. He saw a Shadow Wing courier twice without knowing what they were. He will tell the party everything.

npcs.name: Governor Harwick Denn
npcs.description: He was appointed by [[The Trade Compact]] to run Ashford. He has been quietly paying the [[The Ironmasks|Ironmask]] toll from civic funds and recording it as road maintenance. He knows exactly what he has done and spends his evenings convincing himself it was the only reasonable option. He is not wrong that he had no other options. He is wrong that it ends there.
npcs.dm_notes: He has been keeping a personal ledger of every payment — names, dates, amounts. Not as evidence against the Ironmasks. As insurance against the [[The Trade Compact|Trade Compact]] if they ever decide to make him a scapegoat. He hid it behind a false panel in his study. He will not produce it voluntarily, but he will not destroy it either.

log.note: The party confronts Governor Denn with Petra's evidence. He does not deny it. He says: 'What exactly did you expect me to do?' He agrees to stop the civic fund payments going forward. He does nothing else.

Consequence of Maren Vosk (ally): The Ledger responds to the Keep incident by doubling Ironmask patrols in town and imposing a 'security surcharge' on all merchants. She is escalating, not retreating.
Consequence of Maren Vosk (ally): Ironmask escalation — doubled patrols, new surcharge on merchants.
Consequence of Maren Vosk (ally): The Ledger captured — Ironmask operation collapses.

npcs.name: Nessa Crane
npcs.description: She runs the largest trading post in Ashford. She has been paying the [[The Ironmasks|Ironmask]] toll and passing the cost to her suppliers. Her suppliers are starting to push back. She wants the Ironmasks gone but is not willing to be seen working against them until it is safe to do so. She is watching the party's progress carefully.
npcs.dm_notes: One of her suppliers is a shell company controlled by [[The Shadow Wing]]. She did not know that when she signed the contract and has been afraid to back out since. If the party helps her dissolve the arrangement quietly, she becomes a reliable and well-connected ally.

npcs.name: Orvyn Thatch
npcs.description: The town's most reliable gossip. He knows everything that moves through Ashford because he sells everything to everyone. He has been quietly giving discounts to people [[The Ironmasks]] have victimized, which has strained his margins. He is the party's best source of local intelligence.
npcs.dm_notes: He keeps a hidden list of every person the [[The Ironmasks|Ironmasks]] have victimized and what they took — names, dates, amounts. He calls it his debt ledger. He has been building it for eight months, waiting for someone who could actually use it. He will hand it over immediately if asked.

log.note: The party arrives in Ashford and stops at Thatch's General Store. Orvyn gives them a full picture without being asked: the toll, Bram's capture, the Registry situation. He's been waiting for someone to tell.

npcs.name: Petra Holt
npcs.description: She registers every miner, tracks shift schedules, and maintains the payroll ledger for the Vein. She has noticed that payroll deductions for 'safety equipment' don't correspond to any equipment she can see. The [[The Ironmasks|Ironmasks]] are running a payroll skim through the Registry. She has the evidence — and [[Sister Veyne]] is the only person in Ashford she fully trusts.
npcs.dm_notes: She recognized one of the [[The Ironmasks|Ironmask]] collectors as a former Registry clerk she dismissed two years ago. She has not told anyone. She doesn't know if it's relevant, but the coincidence has been bothering her for months. The name is in her employment records.

log.note: Petra Holt shows the party the Registry payroll records. The 'safety equipment' deductions are real deductions against real miner wages. Twelve percent of the payroll is being skimmed through a fictitious expense. She has a complete ledger copy hidden under the Registry floor.

npcs.name: Farlan Dusk
npcs.description: Retired on a hip injury, living in Ashford for three years. He still knows the Greywood better than anyone in the active [[Greywood Rangers]]. He has been watching the [[The Ironmasks|Ironmask]] supply routes out of old habit and has a very good idea of where they go. He will help if asked directly. He doesn't volunteer. [[Thane Bergrak]] is the one person in town he's on speaking terms with from before.
npcs.dm_notes: His hip injury was an [[The Ironmasks|Ironmask]] beating three years ago — before the gang had that name. He has personal history with whoever is running this organization and suspects [[Corvin Ashale]] specifically, based on wagon routes he was tracking at the time. He has no proof and will not say the name unless pushed.

npcs.name: Sister Veyne
npcs.role: Active Greywood Ranger — cleric attached to the patrol
npcs.description: The only active [[Greywood Rangers|Ranger]] currently in Ashford. She has been filing incident reports about [[The Ironmasks]] for two months. The reports go nowhere because they go to [[Governor Harwick Denn]]. She has started filing them with [[The Trade Compact]] instead, which is how [[Edda Sorn]] found out about the payroll skim.
npcs.dm_notes: She has incident reports on every [[The Ironmasks|Ironmask]] action for the past two months, filed in triplicate. One copy went to the Governor's office. One went to the [[The Trade Compact|Trade Compact]]. One she kept. The kept copy has observations that don't appear in the others — she has been more careful about what she writes in official filings.

log.note: Sister Veyne coordinates a simultaneous ranger action on three Ironmask collection points. With the party holding the road north, the operation lands cleanly. Seventeen Ironmasks captured. Maren Vosk's records seized.

npcs.name: Maren Vosk
npcs.description: She runs [[The Ironmasks|Ironmask]] operation in Ashford with ledger-perfect precision. Every toll, every payroll skim, every protection payment is documented. She does not enjoy violence but authorizes it without hesitation when the numbers require it. She knows exactly who the Factor is and considers herself insulated from any consequences. She knows [[Governor Harwick Denn]] won't move against her. She is wrong about what happens next.
npcs.dm_notes: She has her own exit plan — a coded arrangement with a [[The Trade Compact|Trade Compact]] courier to extract her if the operation burns. [[Corvin Ashale]] does not know about it. She would give up his identity before the party finishes the first question, if she genuinely believed it would save her.

log.note: The Ledger's collectors approach the party within two hours of their arrival to assess whether they are merchants subject to the toll. They back down when challenged but take notes.
The Ledger responds to the Keep incident by doubling Ironmask patrols in town and imposing a 'security surcharge' on all merchants. She is escalating, not retreating.

Maren Vosk is captured in the Registry during the raid. Her ledgers are complete. They name Corvin Ashale by initials — 'CA' — in seventeen separate entries as the recipient of skim payments. She will not confirm the name but she won't deny it.

npcs.name: Corvin Ashale
npcs.description: He presents as a successful independent merchant with a modest warehouse near the mine entrance. He is [[The Shadow Wing]]'s operational coordinator for the Ashcroft Vein: he decides what gets skimmed, routes it through [[Maren Vosk]] and [[The Ironmasks]], and ships it out via a tunnel entrance the [[The Trade Compact|Trade Compact]] doesn't know exists. His courier is [[Serev]]. (DM-only)
npcs.dm_notes: The warehouse tunnel exit opens into the basement of a ruined mill two hundred yards north. He has a horse and three days of supplies staged there. He knows enough about [[The Shadow Wing]]'s regional structure to bargain his way to a lighter sentence — but only if he believes he is genuinely going to hang without a deal.

log.note: Consequence of Maren Vosk (ally): The Ledger responds to the Keep incident by doubling Ironmask patrols in town and imposing a 'security surcharge' on all merchants. She is escalating, not retreating.
Consequence of Maren Vosk (ally): Ironmask escalation — doubled patrols, new surcharge on merchants.

Watching the Coldwater tunnel maps, the party realizes Corvin Ashale's warehouse sits directly above the old tunnel exit. The shipping weight discrepancy finally makes sense. He's not moving cargo out. He's moving it down.
Consequence of Maren Vosk (ally): The Ledger captured — Ironmask operation collapses.
Corvin Ashale barricades himself in his warehouse when the Ironmasks fall. He has already sent a coded message via Serev. The Shadow Wing knows the operation is burned. He is waiting for extraction.

npcs.name: Thane Bergrak
npcs.description: He lives in the Greywood and comes to Ashford once a week to sell timber rights and deliver the [[Emerald Circle]]'s opinions on what the miners are doing wrong. His opinions are accurate but his delivery is confrontational. He knows a Circle-maintained passage through the Greywood that avoids the roads. He and [[Farlan Dusk]] have a professional respect built on years of crossing paths in the same forest.
npcs.dm_notes: The [[Emerald Circle]] has already voted to seal the tunnels. Thane is in Ashford to give the party a chance to act before the Circle does. Their deadline is the next new moon. He will not extend it, and he will not tell the party how close the deadline is unless directly asked.

log.note: Thane Bergrak approaches the party on the road to Coldwater. He knows about the unsafe shaft situation. The Emerald Circle has been monitoring the mine's structural deterioration. He shows them the Circle's own survey: three shafts will fail within sixty days. Not might. Will.

npcs.name: Warchief Durnak
npcs.description: His clan has held Greystone Keep for three generations. They have no interest in Ashford's problems but [[The Ironmasks]] have been using the Keep's outer courtyard as a holding area without asking. He has not acted because they haven't been worth the trouble. That calculation is changing.
npcs.dm_notes: He knows the name of the [[The Ironmasks|Ironmask]] officer who negotiated use of his outer courtyard — a man called Renn, who claimed to represent a trade security firm. He will share that name if asked directly, but he will not volunteer it. He has his own reasons for wanting that name on a warrant.

log.note: The party approaches Greystone Keep and requests parley. Durnak meets them at the gate. He is not hostile but he is not friendly. He will release Bram if the party removes the Ironmask presence from his outer courtyard. He gives them three days.

Consequence of Maren Vosk (rival): The Ledger responds to the Keep incident by doubling Ironmask patrols in town and imposing a 'security surcharge' on all merchants. She is escalating, not retreating.
Consequence of Maren Vosk (rival): Ironmask escalation — doubled patrols, new surcharge on merchants.
Consequence of Maren Vosk (rival): The Ledger captured — Ironmask operation collapses.

npcs.name: The Pale Widow
npcs.description: A spirit haunting the Coldwater ruins two miles from Ashford. The miners avoid her on superstition. [[The Ironmasks]] learned to avoid her because three of their scouts went into Coldwater and didn't come back. She has been watching the Vein for longer than the town has existed.
npcs.dm_notes: She is bound to Coldwater by an unfinished accounting — a garrison commander buried the payroll before the flood and never paid his soldiers. The coin is still in a sealed vault under the ruins. If the party finds it and distributes it to the soldiers' descendants, she is released. She knows this and will not mention it unprompted.

log.note: The party approaches Coldwater ruins respectfully. The Pale Widow does not attack. She shows them the garrison archive: maps of a tunnel system predating the mine, running beneath the Vein. One tunnel connects to an exit point near Corvin Ashale's warehouse.

npcs.name: Serev
npcs.description: [[Corvin Ashale]]'s main courier between the Factor's operation and [[The Shadow Wing]]'s distribution network. Goes by multiple names. Was seen near the Registry two nights before the payroll skim started. (DM-only)
npcs.dm_notes: Serev is a courier designation, not a name. There are currently three active Serevs in the region. The one in Ashford has a silver wing brooch and prefers to work at dusk. They made contact with [[The Shadow Wing]]'s regional handler within six hours of [[Corvin Ashale]]'s warehouse being locked.
